known_gaps: The protected Class AB headphone path now passes schematic electrical validation, PCB realization, placement, and endpoint binding with verified LMV321 op-amp, diode, and output-transistor component selections.
The board envelope is intentionally larger for this fixture so realized amplifier block placements remain inside the outline without weakening placement validation for undersized boards.
Routing remains intentionally skipped by fixture policy, but the placement-to-routing handoff no longer reports unresolved endpoint or local-route blocker issues.

The current hard blocker is structural validation after project write: generated schematic label attachment/conflict evidence and generated PCB route/connectivity evidence still need promotion-quality proof.

Output protection remains connectivity-level: fault_protection_status is placeholder_blocked, active fault protection is not implemented, HP_RET-to-LOAD_REF load-return policy is not promoted, and separated LOAD_REF-to-GND net-tie evidence is not proven.

Amplifier output transistor evidence remains warning-only for fabrication promotion: power dissipation, thermal review, safe operating area, and bias-diode thermal placement evidence are still review_required.
Real KiCad ERC/DRC evidence remains skipped until routing policy and validation blockers are resolved.
notes: Optional protected Class AB headphone amplifier fixture that captures the first KiCad-backed evidence gap after schematic alias cleanup, placement fit, and endpoint binding: structural validation before KiCad checks. Configuring KICADAI_KICAD_CLI should not promote this fixture until it reaches kicad_checks. DRC is intentionally not required while the fixture stops before routed PCB validation.
